GT LEARN #691 📝 Stickers to Spell Word HARD Backtracking / DP
exp: 3

📝 Problem Statement

We are given n different types of stickers. Each sticker has a lowercase English word on it. You want to spell out the given string target by cutting individual letters from your collection of stickers and rearranging them. Return the minimum number of stickers needed.

🧠 Solving Approach

Use Backtracking with Memoization. At each step, pick a sticker that contains the first character of the remaining target. Use it to cancel out as many characters as possible, then recurse on the remaining string.

Pruning Only pick stickers that contain the first char of current target.
Memoization Store results for specific remaining target strings to avoid re-calculation.
Base Case If target is empty, return 0.
Time: O(2^N * M) Space: O(2^N)

🧪 Test Cases (click to load)

T1 Standard exp: 3
stickers=["with","example","science"] target="thehat"
T2 Impossible exp: -1
stickers=["notice","possible"] target="basicbasic"
T3 Trivial exp: 1
stickers=["with","example","science"] target="with"
—
📜 Python Code
📊 Visualization — Memoized DFS Backtracking
🏷️ Available Stickers
🎯 Current Target Remaining (see each c removed live in the inner for c in s loop)
📚 Recursion Stack
Output (Min Stickers)
0/0 INIT
←→ 100%
